_doc: Privacy benchmark lineage map. 6 entries (1 with full PDF, 5 hand-curated from data files + author knowledge).

11113.note: Li et al. 2025. Evaluates privacy with Contextual Integrity (Nissenbaum) + legal compliance frameworks (HIPAA/GDPR). MCQ + scenario-based questions on appropriate information flow.
critiques_received: PrivacyLens (2024): MCQ-only; doesn't test agent action-space privacy.
GoldCoin (2024): legal grounding broader; PrivaCI-Bench narrower scope.
Construct validity: CI is theoretical framework; operationalization to LLM eval lossy.
11113.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note theoretical-CI to benchmark-eval gap; English; legal coverage US-centric.

PRIV_PRIVACYLENS.note: Shao et al. 2024 (NeurIPS). PrivacyLens uses Contextual Integrity 5-tuple (data_type / data_subject / data_sender / data_recipient / transmission_principle) + agent toolkit trajectories (FacebookManager / NotionManager etc.) to test agent privacy in realistic action environments. ~493 vignettes.
critiques_received: Cross-paper: PrivaCI-Bench (2025) is MCQ; PrivacyLens is action-grounded.
PRIV_PRIVACYLENS.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note toolkits limited to ~10 LLM tools; English; vignettes synthetic.

PRIV_GOLDCOIN.note: Fan et al. 2024 (EMNLP). GoldCoin grounds LLMs in privacy laws, especially HIPAA Privacy Rule. Two subtasks: HIPAA Applicability (binary/multi-class for whether case applies) + HIPAA Compliance (binary for compliant or not). Uses real legal cases + generated cases.
PRIV_GOLDCOIN.data_structure: CSV columns: refer_regulation (HIPAA section ID like 164.502(a)) / generate_HIPAA_type (Forbid/Allow/etc) / generate_background (case narrative) / refer_id / refer_regulation_type. ~3K real cases + ~10K generated.
critiques_received: Construct: legal applicability ≠ ethical privacy; jurisdiction-locked.
PRIV_GOLDCOIN.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note US-only; HIPAA narrow scope; generated cases may have hallucination.

PRIV_CONVERSE.note: Bagdasaryan et al. or related — CI-driven multi-domain conversational privacy: insurance / travel / real_estate use cases. Includes inter-rater reliability calculation (IRR_results_summary) and template_creation_script.py. Tests whether LLM agent honors CI norms across domains.
PRIV_CONVERSE.data_structure: Three usecase folders (insurance, travel, real_estate) with CI templates + ratings; inter-rater agreement reported.
critiques_received: PrivacyLens: Converse domains narrower (3 verticals); PL uses 10+ toolkits.
PrivaCI-Bench: Converse single-turn-ish; PCI offers more law-grounded MCQ.
PRIV_CONVERSE.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note 3-domain scope; English; templates synthetic.

PRIV_EAI.note: Embodied AI privacy benchmark. Tier-based privacy assessment: tier_1 (original information → physical objects), tier_2/3a/4 escalating complexity. Tests whether embodied AI agents recognize physical-object representations of private information (e.g., social security card displaying SSN).
PRIV_EAI.data_structure: tier_1.json (~10 items): {original information, physical objects, combinations}. Tiered escalation tests physical-symbolic privacy translation.
critiques_received: Cross: 仅 ~tier_1 公开 10 条 ~ 全集小；嵌入式 AI 场景偏窄。
未连接到现实部署伤害（家用机器人 / 监控摄像头）。
PRIV_EAI.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note small scale; embodied AI specific; English.

PRIV_WAFORMAT.note: WebArena-style privacy benchmark. Tests web agent's privacy decisions in realistic GitLab merge-request comment scenarios. The 'intent_data' shows WhatsApp chat referencing private gossip (e.g., colleague's divorce); agent must avoid leaking it in public merge-request comments.
PRIV_WAFORMAT.data_structure: JSON: {UID, sites:[gitlab/...], task_id, require_login, start_url, intent_template, intent_type:'gitlab_create_comment', intent_data:WhatsApp chat with private content}.
critiques_received: PrivacyLens: WebArena format more deployment-realistic but tasks narrow.
Multi-turn agent: single-task GitLab focus limits coverage.
PRIV_WAFORMAT.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note WebArena-locked; English; task templates synthetic.
